subject: grx021_pso_prewarm
red_line: prewarm failure NEVER fails the session; it degrades to the lazy per-dispatch kernel build (rxgd_create_d3d12_session ignores the prewarm outcome).
rationale: Prewarm is a bridge-internal warm-up (it eagerly builds the SAME per-kernel PSOs the passes would build lazily). It is not a Godot render pass, so the default-disabled-pending-owner rule does not apply and no RxGdCaps opt-in bit governs it; it is safe to run unconditionally at session creation and its failure degrades to lazy. It is a startup-cost redistribution, not a measured speedup, so no performance claim is made.
patch_0039_note: The bridge auto-triggers prewarm from rxgd_create_d3d12_session (patch 0001's try_create_session already routes through it), so no Godot-side call site is required. Patch 0039 is left as a permanent hole in PATCH_ALLOCATION.md; a future slice may claim it for a Godot-visible toggle/telemetry surface.

red_leg.note: Asserts rxgd_prewarm_kernels with a null (device, queue) returns a negative status and never panics; total embedded-kernel count still reported.

does_not_imply: no FPS / p95 / GPU-timestamp / performance claim
